purpose: Shared protocol describing how every Synapse command resolves the {id} parameter, claims a dashboard, derives swarm status, and reads dashboard data. Defines the data path map (initialization.json/logs.json/progress/{task_id}.json), the parameter-parsing rules for first-positional {id}, the read-vs-write resolution rules, the selectDashboard() claiming algorithm with mandatory pre-assigned-dashboard isolation, the deriveOverallStatus() algorithm from progress files, the reading-agent-data merge pattern, derived stats formulas, IDE dashboard reservation, and worker-routing rule. Read by every command that touches dashboard data.
gotchas: Pre-assigned dashboards (system prompt `DASHBOARD ID:` directive) are ABSOLUTE and UNCONDITIONAL — agents have NO read or write access to any other dashboard, enforced by the enforce-dashboard-isolation.sh PreToolUse hook via SYNAPSE_DASHBOARD_ID env var.
Auto-archive on a non-empty pre-assigned dashboard is automatic and non-destructive — never ask the user for confirmation.
There is NO auto-selection or scanning algorithm — every agent MUST have a dashboard via system prompt or --dashboard flag, otherwise ask the user.
The `ide` dashboard is permanently reserved for the IDE agent — swarm agents must never use it.
Status is DERIVED from progress files, never stored in initialization.json. The master never maintains counters — derivation is the dashboard client's job.
When an agent has no progress file yet, status defaults to `pending` (not missing/null) — do not skip it.
Mix of completed and pending without any in_progress still counts as `in_progress` overall (dispatch is ongoing).
Workers must use the absolute progress path the master tells them — `{tracker_root}/dashboards/{id}/progress/{task_id}.json` — never construct a relative dashboards/ path from CWD.
patterns: First-positional-{id} parameter rule — non-flag, non-task-id strings are consumed as dashboard IDs (6-char hex, `ide`, or legacy `dashboardN`).
Resolution order: (1) pre-assigned via system prompt `DASHBOARD ID:` (highest priority) → (2) explicit --dashboard flag → (no fallback).

Archive-then-clear-then-verify when reusing a pre-assigned dashboard with prior data: copy to Archive/{date}_{task_name}/, rm -f progress/*.json, reset logs.json, verify ls is empty before writing initialization.json.
Status derivation algorithm — read agents[] from initialization.json, build {taskId: progressData} map from progress/, fold per-agent statuses into overall status using fixed condition table.

Two-source merge pattern: initialization.json provides {id, title, wave, layer, directory, depends_on}; progress/{id}.json provides {status, started_at, completed_at, summary, stage, message, milestones, deviations, logs}.
Wave status is derived per-wave: completed if ALL agents terminal, in_progress if ANY active, pending otherwise.
conventions: Path tables (`Path | Contents | Written By`) for both per-dashboard and global directories.
Resolution-order numbered sections with explicit 'No auto-selection. No scanning. No fallback.' callout.
Status-derivation condition table mapping aggregate state → overall status.
